keyPoints: A transaction is initiated by a user creating a digital request.
The transaction is broadcast to a peer-to-peer network for validation.
Validated transactions are grouped into a new block and added to the existing blockchain.
commonMistakes: Believing a transaction is confirmed as soon as it is sent; it requires network validation.
Confusing the mempool (where unconfirmed transactions wait) with a confirmed block on the chain.
Assuming all transactions are permanently immutable; while extremely difficult, chain reorganizations can theoretically alter recent history.
